confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Passages advanced to evidence-review after online scholarly verification (GHDI). Chain remains thin; physical edition verification and expanded sourcing required before score can advance.

reviewNote: Score value 3/5 defensible: collective-immortality variable scored at 3 (moderate-high) rather than maximum because the evidence chain documents the subordination mechanism but not the full immortality discourse present in other passages (e.g., blood-and-soil texts). Confidence 0.65 is proportionate. Advancing from argument-review to score-review: argument-validity check confirms the passage (NSDAP 1933 appeal) directly supports the collective-immortality interpretation at this score value.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Passages advanced to evidence-review. Score reflects strong sacred political order strength consistent with case role; physical edition verification and expanded sourcing required before scholarly use.

reviewNote: Score value 5/5 defensible: maximum sacred-political-order-strength is strongly supported by the chain — both NSDAP founding texts and the institutional self-preservation mechanism (Wehrmacht oath, one-party law) document an order that commanded total collective identity and obligation. Confidence 0.60 is lower than expected given the strong evidence; the uncertainty factors (single-claim and physical edition pending) are real. Ready for human-reviewed once physical edition verification is complete.

confidence.label: high
confidence.rationale: Minimum corrigibility: both legal instruments directly eliminate the institutional preconditions for order self-correction — unconditional personal oath forecloses military dissent; single-party law forecloses political competition. Score of 1 (not 0) preserves the possibility that informal networks and internal party factionalism provided marginal corrigibility channels, but the constitutional-legal architecture is unambiguous.

reviewNote: Score value 1/5 defensible: minimum corrigibility is strongly supported by simultaneous institutional mechanisms (personal loyalty oath eliminating command accountability; one-party law eliminating political correction). Confidence 0.82 is the highest in the dataset and is justified — both mechanisms are directly documented in the evidence chain and mutually reinforcing. Ready for human-reviewed: this is the strongest-evidenced score in the gold cases.
